record.field: LimeSurvey is a self-hosted platform for building advanced surveys, forms, questionnaires and assessments, and collecting structured responses at scale.

LimeSurvey is a self-hosted platform for building advanced surveys, forms, questionnaires and assessments, and collecting structured responses at scale.

Written in PHP, LimeSurvey lets you design sophisticated questionnaires with conditional logic, quotas and dozens of question types, then publish them to unlimited respondents while keeping every answer on your own infrastructure. It is a mature choice for HR, market and academic research, customer feedback, education and internal data collection, where privacy and full control over sensitive response data matter.

Key features
  • Dozens of question types with branching logic, quotas and validation
  • Unlimited surveys and responses, with anonymous or tracked participation
  • Participant management, invitations, reminders and token-based access
  • Statistics, charts and exports to CSV, Excel, PDF, SPSS and R
  • Multi-language surveys, custom themes and a template editor
  • Optional response encryption, REST/RPC API and granular user permissions
Best for

HR teams, researchers, educators and organizations that need a powerful, privacy-respecting survey tool for structured data collection while keeping respondent data self-hosted. Serve it over HTTPS and take care with response exports.

services: limesurvey: image: martialblog/limesurvey:6-apache restart: unless-stopped environment: - DB_TYPE=mysql - DB_HOST=db - DB_PORT=3306 - DB_NAME={{ db_name }} - DB_USERNAME={{ db_user }} - DB_PASSWORD={{ db_password }} - ADMIN_USER={{ admin_user }} - ADMIN_NAME=Administrator - ADMIN_PASSWORD={{ admin_password }} - ADMIN_EMAIL={{ admin_email }} - PUBLICURL=https://{{ domain }} volumes: - ./upload:/var/www/html/upload/surveys depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: mariadb:11 restart: unless-stopped environment: - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD={{ db_root_password }} - MYSQL_DATABASE={{ db_name }} - MYSQL_USER={{ db_user }} - MYSQL_PASSWORD={{ db_password }} volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/mysql healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - healthcheck.sh --connect --innodb_initialized interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 30s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' expose: - '3306' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d db # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
Name of the MariaDB database LimeSurvey uses.
Password for the LimeSurvey application database user.
Root password for the MariaDB server.
Login name for the initial LimeSurvey administrator.
Password for the initial LimeSurvey administrator account.
Contact email for the initial LimeSurvey administrator.
